sections.text: 4 Section 7.3 Labelling of Non-Retail Containers Information required in Section 7 of this Standard and Sections 4.1 to 4.8 of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), and, if necessary, storage instructions, shall be given either on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification, and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents. The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to cheeses in brine, intended for direct consumption or further processing, in conformity with the description in Section  2 of this standard. Subject  to the provisions of this group standard, Codex Alimentarius standards for individual varieties of  cheeses in brine may contain provisions which are more specific than those in this standard.

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Cheeses in brine are semi-hard to soft ripened cheeses in conformity with the General Standard for Cheese  (CXS 283-1978). The body has a white to yellowish colour and a compact texture suitable for slicing, with none to few mechanical openings. The cheeses have no actual rind and have been ripened and preserved in brine until delivered to, or prepacked for, the consumer. Certain individual cheeses in brine contain specific herbs and spices as part of their identity.

Certain individual cheeses in brine contain specific herbs and spices as part of their identity.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Only those additive classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified. Only certain acidity regulators in Table  3 of the General Standard for Food Additives  (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this standard. Additive functional class Justified use Colours - Bleaching agents - Acidity regulators X Stabilizers - Thickeners - Emulsifiers - Antioxidants - Preservatives - Foaming agents - Anticaking agents - Packaging gas - X The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified.

sections.title: CONTAMINANTS
sections.text: The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants that are specified for the product in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The milk used in the manufacture of the products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels for contaminants and toxins specified for milk by the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with the maximum residue limits for veterinary drug residues and pesticides established for milk by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Milk and Milk Products (CXC 57-2004) and other relevant Codex Alimentarius texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice. The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Name of the food
sections.text: The name of the food shall be ‘cheese in brine’. However, the word ‘cheese in brine’ may be omitted in the designation of an individual cheese in brine variety reserved by a Codex Alimentarius standard for individual cheese in brine, and, in the absence thereof, a variety name specified in the national legislation of the country in which the product is sold, provided that the omission does not create an erroneous impression regarding the character of the food.

sections.title: Declaration of milkfat content
sections.text: The milkfat content shall be declared in a manner found acceptable in the country of sale to the final consumer, either (i) as a percentage by mass, (ii) as a percentage of fat in dry matter (FDM), or (iii) in grams per serving as quantified in the label provided that the number of servings is stated. Additionally, the following terms may be used: High fat (if the content of FDM is above or equal to 60%); Full fat (if the content of FDM is above or equal to 45% and less than 60%) Medium fat (if the content of FDM is above or equal to 25% and less than 45%) Partially skimmed (if the content of FDM is above or equal to 10% and less than 25%) Skim (if the content of FDM is less than 10%)

sections.title: Sampling
sections.text: Special requirements for cheese in brine: A representative piece of cheese is placed on a cloth or on a sheet of absorbent paper for 5 min to 10 min. A slice of 2 cm–3 cm is cut off and sent to the laboratory in a sealed insulated box for analysis.

sentences.original: Only those additive classes indicated as justified in the table below may be used for the product categories specified.

prefilter.reason: Contains normative language indicating justification, relevant for normative analysis.

classification.sentence: The use of additives belonging to the class is technologically justified.
classification.function: Expression of allowance based on technological justification
classification.pattern: Subject (The use of additives belonging to the class) + Predicate (is technologically justified) expressing permission based on justification

components.subject: The use of additives belonging to the class

classification.justification: The sentence expresses permission conditioned on a technological justification but does not use typical modal verbs or imperative structures. It states a justification as basis for allowance without an explicit modal verb, differing from known templates. Therefore, it is a new pattern reflecting permission by justification.
notes: The sentence implies permission by stating technological justification but does not explicitly grant permission with modal verbs such as 'may'.
Ambiguity exists whether this is a condition or an expression of permission; interpreted as permission due to the positive evaluative predicate.

The sentence structure includes an introductory purpose phrase followed by a prescriptive main clause. No known templates match closely, so a new pattern reflecting this structure is created.
notes: The sentence combines normative obligation with purpose indication in a complex sentence; no direct template found in catalog.

sentences.original: The name of the food shall be ‘cheese in brine’.

prefilter.reason: contains normative lexical cues

classification.sentence: However, the word cheese in brine may be omitted in the designation of an individual cheese in brine variety reserved by a Codex Alimentarius standard for individual cheese in brine, and, in the absence thereof, a variety name specified in the national legislation of the country in which the product is sold, provided that the omission does not create an erroneous impression regarding the character of the food.
classification.category: Naming and Labeling Exceptions and Conditions
classification.pattern: Subject + may be omitted + condition clause introduced by 'provided that'

sentences.original: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).

classification.sentence: The labelling of non-retail containers should be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
classification.function: Mandate to comply with a specified standard
classification.pattern: Subject + modal (should) + be + in accordance with + reference to a standard

components.subject: The labelling of non-retail containers
components.prescribed_action: be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021)

classification.justification: The sentence issues an obligation expressed with 'should' regarding compliance with a specific standard. The structure involves a subject and a compliance mandate referencing an external standard. Since no existing templates were matched and the structure is specific, it was classified as a new pattern with obligation modality and mandate communicative function.
notes: The use of 'should' here conveys a normative expectation often treated as obligation in regulatory text.
No exact template was found for 'should be in accordance with' phrasing referencing a standard.

pages.summary: Página contém título principal do documento e cabeçalho identificador, sem seções ou texto interno adicional.

pages.title: CXS 208-1999
pages.summary: A página apresenta dois títulos principais em caixa alta sem numeração que organizam o conteúdo: '2022 AMENDMENT' e 'PAGE LOCATION TEXT IN PREVIOUS VERSION  TEXT IN AMENDED VERSION'. O texto associado está corretamente segmentado conforme os títulos. Não há indicações de continuidade com outras páginas.

pages.title: CXS 208-1999
pages.summary: A página contém o título principal do documento e cabeçalho de página. A estrutura organizacional foi bem definida com quatro níveis principais e alguns subtítulos no nível 2. Os textos estão corretamente associados aos seus títulos. Não há indicação de continuidade com páginas anteriores ou seguintes.

pages.title: CXS 208-1999
pages.summary: A página contém o cabeçalho e o título do documento claramente indicados. A estrutura principal da página é baseada em títulos numéricos de nível 1 (5 a 8). O item 7 LABELLING possui subtítulos no nível 2 (7.1, 7.2, 7.3). Os textos estão associados corretamente aos seus títulos, respeitando a hierarquia e sequência da página. Não há continuidade com páginas anteriores ou seguintes neste conteúdo.
